sections.title: Substrate Propagation Map
sections.text:
Every Q2 substrate choice --- Matter, Mind, Information, Mathematical, Relational, Brute Primitive --- carried through all 12 tree levels. At each node: can this substrate answer the question? Published theories embedded at each step. The tree isn\'t rigged. Watch where each choice breaks on its own terms.
::::
:::::::::::::::::::: substrate-legend
:::: sub-leg
::: {.sub-dot style="background:var(--matter)"}
:::

sections.text:
::::

::: {.sub-leg style="color:var(--pass)"}
✓ PASS
:::
::: {.sub-leg style="color:var(--strain)"}
⚠ STRAIN
:::
::: {.sub-leg style="color:var(--fail)"}
✕ FAIL
:::
::: {.sub-leg style="color:var(--stop)"}
■ TERMINAL
:::
:::::::
::::::::::::::::::::
::: thesis

sections.title: The Propagation Principle
sections.text:
Whatever you choose at Q2 becomes your substrate. That substrate must answer **every subsequent question** --- Q3 through Q12 --- without contradiction. Where it can\'t answer, that\'s a **blank**. Where it contradicts itself, that\'s a **death**. Where it answers only by borrowing from another substrate\'s vocabulary, that\'s **strain**. The accumulated cost of \"strain\" positions becomes unsustainable --- typically by Q6--Q8.
At each node, **published secular theories** are embedded --- showing what physics already proved and what questions it left front and center. Those open questions converge on the same shape: the form of the boundary conditions (BC1--BC8).
:::

sections.title: Published Theories
sections.text:
:::
::: p-item
**Shannon (1948)** --- Formally defined distinction as 1 bit of information.
:::
::: p-item
**Bateson (1972)** --- \"A difference that makes a difference.\"
:::
::::::
:::::: {.panel .question-panel}
::: panel-label

sections.title: Questions Left Open
sections.text:
:::
::: p-item
What IS a distinction? Physical thing? Mental event? Information?
:::
::: p-item
→ Forces Q2. Every substrate must now explain its own nature.
:::

:::::::::::

sections.title: Matter
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .strain}
⚠ CIRCULAR
:::
::: track-text
Distinction = property of matter. But matter hasn\'t been established yet. **Presupposes what it needs to explain.**
:::
:::::::
::::::: track
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--mind)"}
:::

sections.title: Mind
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .strain}
⚠ CIRCULAR
:::
::: track-text
Distinction = property of mind. But mind hasn\'t been established yet. **Same circularity.**
:::
:::::::
::::::: track
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--info)"}
:::

sections.title: Information
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .pass}
✓ PASS
:::
::: track-text
**Definitional identity.** Distinction ≡ 1 bit. Shannon formalized this. No circularity --- distinction IS information by definition.
:::
:::::::
::::::: track
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--math)"}
:::

sections.title: Mathematical
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .strain}
⚠ VARIANT
:::
::: track-text
Math relations ARE informational structures. **Collapses into Information** under analysis. Not independent.
:::
:::::::
::::::: track
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--rel)"}
:::

sections.title: Relational
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .strain}
⚠ COLLAPSES
:::
::: track-text
Relations without relata = Q2-D (dead). Relations WITH content = information. **Ladyman/French structural realism** either collapses or reduces.
:::
:::::::
::::::: track
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--brute)"}
:::

sections.title: Brute
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .fail}
✕ FAIL
:::
::: track-text
A distinction with no content is indistinguishable from no distinction. **Violates Q1.**
:::
:::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::: panels
:::::::: {.panel .theory-panel}
::: panel-label

sections.title: Published Theories --- The God-Shaped Holes Begin
sections.text:
:::
::: p-item
**Wheeler (1990)** --- \"It from Bit\": physical reality derives from binary distinctions.
:::
::: p-item
**Landauer (1961)** --- Information erasure costs physical energy (kT ln 2). Information is physical.
:::
::: p-item
**Shannon (1948)** --- Information = reduction of uncertainty between distinguishable states.
:::
::: p-item
**Holographic Principle (Bekenstein/Hawking)** --- S = A/4ℓ²ₚ. Information content is the fundamental thing; geometry is derived.
:::
::::::::
:::::::: {.panel .question-panel}
::: panel-label

sections.title: Questions These Theories Left Open
sections.text:
:::
::: p-item
Wheeler: **Where does the bit come from?** What instantiates it?
:::
::: p-item
Landauer: **Why does information cost energy?** What\'s the deeper coupling?
:::
::: p-item
Holographic: **Why is geometry derived from information** rather than the reverse?
:::
::: p-item
→ All three questions point to: **the substrate that carries information.**
:::
::::::::
:::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

::: node-question

:::
::: node-arrow
▼

sections.title: Matter
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .strain}
⚠ STRAIN
:::
::: track-text
Explains structure well (laws of physics). But carrying circular Q2 forward. Can describe order but not explain WHY matter has it.
:::
:::::::
::::::: track
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--mind)"}
:::

sections.title: Mind
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .strain}
⚠ STRAIN
:::
::: track-text
Consciousness imposes structure. But on what? Still circular. And solipsism risk: whose mind?
:::
:::::::
::::::: track
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--info)"}
:::

sections.title: Information
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .pass}
✓ PASS
:::
::: track-text
**Shannon entropy:** random info = max entropy = zero usable content. Structure is necessary for meaning. Coherence measure C\[χ\] ≥ 0.
:::
:::::::
::::::: track
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--math)"}
:::

sections.title: Mathematical
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .strain}
⚠ STRAIN
:::
::: track-text
Math IS structure. But this is redundant --- \"structure requires structure.\" No explanatory power added.
:::
:::::::
::::::: {.track .eliminated}
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--rel)"}
:::

sections.title: Relational
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .dead}

sections.title: COLLAPSED
sections.text:
:::
::: track-text
Absorbed into Information at Q2.
:::
:::::::
::::::: {.track .eliminated}
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--brute)"}
:::

sections.title: Brute
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .dead}

sections.title: Published Theories
sections.text:
:::
::: p-item
**Kolmogorov Complexity** --- Shortest program length. Ordered systems are compressible.
:::
::: p-item
**Tononi IIT (2004)** --- Φ measures integrated information. Order = irreducible whole \> sum of parts.
:::
::: p-item
**Friston Free Energy (2010)** --- Living systems minimize surprise = maintain internal order.
:::
::: p-item
**Second Law of Thermodynamics** --- Closed systems trend toward disorder. Order requires explanation.
:::
::::::::
:::::::: {.panel .question-panel}
::: panel-label
Questions Left Open → BC-Shaped
:::
::: p-item
Tononi: Φ measures coherence but **what generates it?** → BC2 (external source)
:::
::: p-item
Friston: Systems minimize surprise but **what set the prior?** → BC1 (terminal observer)
:::
::: p-item
Second Law: Entropy increases --- **where does order come from?** → BC6 (infinite energy source)
:::
::: p-item
Kolmogorov: Compressibility exists --- **why is the universe compressible at all?** → Q4
:::
::::::::
:::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {.node q="4"}
:::::: {.node-header onclick="toggleNode(this)"}
::: node-qid
Q4
:::
::: node-question
What GROUNDS the structure? --- THE GREAT FORK
:::
::: node-arrow
▼
:::
::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: node-body
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: tracks
::::::: track
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--matter)"}

sections.title: Matter
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .stop}
■ TERMINAL
:::
::: track-text
**Brute fact.** \"The laws are what they are.\" Logically coherent. Epistemically closed. **Stops here.** No further questions answerable.
:::
:::::::
::::::: track
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--mind)"}
:::

sections.title: Mind
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .strain}
⚠ STRAIN
:::
::: track-text
Mind grounds structure → idealism. But which mind? Solipsism. And what grounds the mind? Circular or regress.
:::
:::::::
::::::: track
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--info)"}
:::

sections.title: Information
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .pass}
✓ PASS
:::
::: track-text
**Self-grounding.** The informational substrate grounds itself --- its \"why\" and \"what\" are identical. Mathematical necessity. Forces Q5.
:::
:::::::
::::::: track
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--math)"}
:::

sections.title: Mathematical
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .strain}
⚠ STRAIN
:::
::: track-text
Tegmark\'s Mathematical Universe. But how do abstract objects cause anything? **Causation gap.** Math describes; doesn\'t instantiate.
:::
:::::::
::::::: {.track .eliminated}
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--rel)"}
:::

sections.title: Relational
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .dead}

sections.title: DEAD
sections.text:
:::
::: track-text
---
:::
:::::::
::::::: {.track .eliminated}
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--brute)"}
:::

sections.title: Brute
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .dead}

sections.title: DEAD
sections.text:
:::
::: track-text
---
:::
:::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::: panels
:::::::: {.panel .theory-panel}
::: panel-label
Published Theories --- Secular Physics\' Own Stopping Point
:::
::: p-item
**Hawking/Mlodinow (2010)** --- \"Model-dependent realism.\" Physics describes, doesn\'t explain ground.
:::
::: p-item
**Tegmark (2014)** --- Mathematical Universe Hypothesis. Reality IS math. But causation gap.
:::
::: p-item
**Penrose (2004)** --- Three-world model: physical, mental, mathematical. No single ground.
:::
::: p-item
**Cosmological Argument** --- Regress must terminate. What terminates it?
:::
::::::::
:::::::: {.panel .question-panel}
::: panel-label
Questions Left Open → These ARE the Form of God
:::
::: p-item
Why these laws and not others? → **Self-grounding, non-arbitrary substrate**
:::
::: p-item
Why is math \"unreasonably effective\"? (Wigner 1960) → **Because math describes the substrate**
:::
::: p-item
What breaks the regress? → **Self-reference. Self-grounding entity.**
:::
::: p-item
Every open question at Q4 has the SHAPE of BC1 + Q5 properties.
:::
::::::::
:::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: {.node q="5"}
:::::: {.node-header onclick="toggleNode(this)"}
::: node-qid
Q5
:::
::: node-question
What are the necessary properties of a self-grounding substrate?
:::
::: node-arrow
▼
:::

sections.text:

sections.text:

Cannot derive. No ground to derive from.

sections.title: Mind
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .fail}
✕ FAIL
:::
::: track-text
Can\'t derive non-temporal, non-material, non-deceptive from \"mind.\" Mind is temporal. Mind can deceive. **Properties conflict.**
:::
:::::::
::::::: track
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--info)"}
:::

sections.title: Information
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .pass}
✓ PASS
:::
::: track-text
**Self-consistent → maximally coherent → non-deceptive → generative → non-temporal → non-material.** All derived, none assumed.
:::
:::::::
::::::: track
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--math)"}
:::

sections.title: Mathematical
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .fail}
✕ FAIL
:::
::: track-text
Math is non-temporal, non-material ✓. But NOT generative. Abstract objects don\'t cause anything. **Causation gap kills it.**
:::
:::::::
::::::: {.track .eliminated}
:::: track-label
::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--rel)"}
:::

sections.title: Relational
sections.text:

sections.title: DEAD
sections.text:

::: {.track-dot style="background:var(--brute)"}
:::

sections.title: Brute
sections.text:
::::
::: {.track-status .dead}

sections.title: Published Theories --- Properties Match
sections.text:
:::
::: p-item
**Gödel (1931)** --- Self-referencing systems produce undecidable truths. Self-grounding must be richer than formal systems.
:::
::: p-item
**Tarski (1936)** --- Truth about a system can\'t be defined within it. Self-grounding requires transcendence.
:::
::: p-item
**Noether (1918)** --- Conservation laws follow from symmetries. Coherence conserved if ground has right symmetries.
:::
:::::::
::::::::: {.panel .question-panel}
::: panel-label
Questions Left Open → These ARE God\'s Attributes
:::
::: p-item
Non-temporal entity that generates temporal reality → **Eternal**
:::
::: p-item
Non-material source of material → **Spirit / Immaterial**
:::
::: p-item
Maximally coherent → **Omniscient** (no internal incoherence)
:::
::: p-item
Non-deceptive → **Truth itself** (John 14:6)
:::
::: p-item
Generative → **Creator**
:::
:::::::::
:::::::::::::::
:::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::

sections.title: Published Theories --- Measurement Problem
sections.text:
:::
::: p-item
**Wheeler Delayed Choice (1978)** --- Observation determines which-path retroactively. Observer participation real.
:::
::: p-item
**Born Rule (1926)** --- Probability amplitudes squared give outcomes UPON measurement.
:::
::: p-item
**Wigner\'s Friend (1961)** --- Where does the observer chain end? Who collapses whom?
:::
::: p-item
**PEAR Lab (Jahn/Dunne, 6.35σ, 2.5M trials)** --- Consciousness correlates with RNG outcomes.
:::
::::::::
::::::: {.panel .question-panel}
::: panel-label
Questions Left Open → BC1, BC3
:::
::: p-item
If observer is required, **who observed the Big Bang?** → BC1 (Terminal Observer)
:::
::: p-item
Can the observer be inside the system it observes? → BC3 (Orthogonality)
:::
::: p-item
Does observation chain terminate or regress? → Q7
:::

sections.title: Published Theories
sections.text:
:::
::: p-item
**Von Neumann Chain (1932)** --- Measurement requires observer → observer requires observer → where does it end?
:::
::: p-item
**Penrose-Hameroff Orch-OR** --- Consciousness as quantum gravity collapse. Terminal? Debated.
:::
::: p-item
**GCP Network (6σ, 325+ events)** --- Collective consciousness correlates with global events.
:::
:::::::
::::::: {.panel .question-panel}
::: panel-label
Questions Left Open → BC1 + BC4
:::
::: p-item
Von Neumann: **The chain MUST terminate** or nothing is ever actually observed.
:::
::: p-item
What are the properties of the terminal observer? → Q5 properties (already derived)
:::
::: p-item
How many terminal observers needed? → BC4 (N=3, pending stronger proof)

sections.title: Published Theories Across Q8--Q12
sections.text:
:::
::: p-item
**Penrose (1989)** --- Emperor\'s New Mind: Consciousness noncomputable. Points to BC1.
:::
::: p-item
**Bekenstein Bound** --- Maximum information in region. Points to BC7 (info conservation).
:::
::: p-item
**No-Cloning Theorem** --- Quantum info can\'t be copied. Points to BC7 (soul uniqueness).
:::
::: p-item
**Boltzmann Brains / Fine-Tuning** --- Points to BC6 (infinite energy source) and non-accidental order.
:::
::: p-item
**Bell\'s Theorem (1964)** --- No local hidden variables. Nonlocality real. Points to BC3 (transcendence).
:::
:::::::::
:::::::::: {.panel .question-panel}
::: panel-label
Every Open Question → Boundary Condition
:::
::: p-item
Hard Problem of Consciousness (Chalmers) → BC1: consciousness requires terminal observer
:::
::: p-item
Arrow of Time → BC6: entropy reversal requires external input
:::
::: p-item
Measurement Problem → BC3: observer must be orthogonal to system
:::
::: p-item
Free Will vs Determinism → BC5 + BC8: superposition until voluntary collapse
:::
::: p-item
Moral Realism → Q10: sign structure σ = ±1 is structural, not cultural
:::
::: p-item
**Every unsolved problem in physics has the shape of a boundary condition.**
:::
::::::::::
::::::::::::::::::

::::: {.mode-cell .m1}
::: mc-label
M1 → Repentance + Grace
:::
::: mc-text

:::
:::::
::::: {.mode-cell .m2}

:::

:::

:::
:::::

M4 → Communion
:::
::: mc-text
Prayer/sacrament = coupling maintenance (Q12)
:::

sections.title: Final Scoreboard: Where Each Substrate Died
Matter
Mind
Information
Mathematical
Relational

equations: Distinction = property of matter. But matter hasn\'t been established yet. **Presupposes what it needs to explain.**
Distinction = property of mind. But mind hasn\'t been established yet. **Same circularity.**
How many terminal observers needed? → BC4 (N=3, pending stronger proof)
Moral Realism → Q10: sign structure σ = ±1 is structural, not cultural
